steps.title: Use the cone volume formula
steps.content: For a cone, volume is $V=\frac{1}{3}\pi r^2h$. The radius is $6$ cm and the height is $13$ cm, so substitute those values directly.

steps.title: Set up
steps.content: The volume of a cone is $$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$$ Substitute $r = 6$ and $h = 13$: $$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi (6)^2(13)$$
